name: The crucifixion
notes: A fourfold Gospel harmony of the crucifixion of the Lord Jesus Christ, told by Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. The shared core runs pair-by-pair across all four: the place of a skull (Golgotha, called Calvary in Luke), the crucifying between two malefactors on either side, the parting of the garments and casting of lots, the title set up over Him proclaiming THE KING OF THE JEWS, and the loud cry as He gave up the ghost. The synoptics together record the mocking and railing of those that passed by, the darkness from the sixth to the ninth hour, the rending of the veil of the temple, the centurion's confession, and the women beholding afar off. Each evangelist keeps complementary detail from his own vantage: Matthew the earthquake and the saints raised, Mark the third hour, Luke the penitent thief and the word of forgiveness, and John his nearer vantage at the cross with the soldiers, the seamless coat, and the women standing by. John's unique matter (the mother and the beloved disciple, "It is finished," the unbroken bones, and the pierced side) is left unlinked here, belonging to a separate prophecy weave.
